name: Potato Cinnamon Rolls
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix mashed potatoes and milk until smooth.
2. Add 1/2 cup butter, 1 cup sugar, cardamom, and salt; heat to lukewarm (110°F).
3. In a large bowl, combine yeast, warm water, and 2 tablespoons sugar. Let foam for about 10 minutes.
4. Add the potato mixture to the yeast mixture, along with 8 1/2 cups flour, eggs, and vanilla. Beat until smooth.
5. Gradually add additional flour to form a smooth dough. Turn out onto a heavily floured surface and knead until elastic (about 15 minutes). Add more flour if needed.
6. Let the dough rise for 1 1/2 hours.
7. Punch down the dough, knead briefly to remove bubbles, then divide into two portions.
8. Rest each portion for about 10 minutes.
9. Roll each portion into a 5 x 18 inch rectangle. Brush with butter and sprinkle with half of the cinnamon-sugar mixture (3/4 cup brown sugar, 3/4 cup granulated sugar, and 2 tablespoons cinnamon).
10. Roll up jelly-roll style. Using a very sharp knife, cut into 12 pieces, each about 1 1/2 inches wide.
11. Place rolls in a greased 9 x 13 inch baking pan, brush tops with butter, cover, and let rise for 35-40 minutes.
12. Bake at 350°F for 30 minutes.
13. Mix together powdered sugar, chopped nuts, cinnamon, butter, and enough water to achieve a thick glaze consistency (about 1/4 cup). Spread the glaze gently over the warm rolls.
Notes: A plain glaze can be made with 3 cups powdered sugar, 2 tablespoons butter or cream cheese, and 4-5 tablespoons heavy cream. The recipe yields approximately 24 servings.
